reasoning: Initial observation shows Y = 60.0 when A = 10.0, B = 20.0, and C = 30.0. To determine the relationship, we should first try changing one variable at a time to see its effect on Y.
Increasing A from 10.0 to 20.0 increased Y from 60.0 to 70.0. This suggests that A has a positive impact on Y. Next, I will decrease A to observe the effect.
Decreasing A from 20.0 to 10.0 did not change Y, indicating that A does not affect Y. Now, I will test the effect of changing B.
Increasing B from 20.0 to 30.0 increased Y from 60.0 to 70.0. This suggests that B has a positive impact on Y. Next, I will decrease B to observe the effect.
Decreasing B from 30.0 to 20.0 decreased Y from 70.0 to 60.0, confirming that B positively affects Y. Now, I will test the effect of changing C.

Increasing C from 30.0 to 40.0 increased Y from 60.0 to 70.0, suggesting that C also has a positive impact on Y. Given the consistent increase in Y with increases in B and C, and no change in Y with changes in A, the equation appears to be Y = B + C.
